items.headline: AMD Q1 2026 print beats (May 5)
items.diff_narrative: **Updated: AMD Q1 2026 print beats — Q1 revenue $10.25B (vs $9.85B consensus, +38% YoY); adjusted EPS $1.37 (vs $1.27 consensus); Data Center $5.78B (+57% YoY) above the $5.56B Street; non-GAAP gross margin 55%; Q2 guidance $11.2B vs Street $10.52B (+50.6% YoY at midpoint); Lisa Su declares Data Center the "primary driver of revenue and earnings growth"; AMD shares +4.4% AH** — Yesterday's report had the print preview (consensus $1.28 / $9.88B, the China-cliff $390M to $100M, Cathie Wood ARK trim). Today the print prints clean above the bar with the Q2 guide print as the operative buyside catalyst; the MI308 China cliff is now in the rearview and Helios + MI400 + MI450 ramp is the forward signal.

items.headline: Anthropic 10-agent financial-services launch (May 5)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Anthropic 10-agent financial-services launch — first single-day frontier-lab vertical-agent product drop with 10 prebuilt agents (pitch builder, meeting preparer, earnings reviewer, model builder, market researcher, valuation reviewer, GL reconciler, month-end closer, statement auditor, KYC screener) on Claude Opus 4.7 (64.37% on Vals AI Finance Agent benchmark, state-of-the-art); native M365 add-ins span Excel + PowerPoint + Word; new MCP connectors land for Dun & Bradstreet, Fiscal AI, Financial Modeling Prep, Guidepoint, IBISWorld, SS&C IntraLinks, Third Bridge, Verisk; Moody's MCP app brings credit ratings + 600M+ public/private company data natively into Claude; testimonials = Citadel, BNY, Carlyle, Mizuho, Travelers, Walleye Capital** — Yesterday's report flagged the Anthropic + Wall Street JV as the equity-template move; today's vertical-agent launch is the consumer-facing edge of the same monetization push and validates the JV-template thesis within the same week.

items.headline: FIS x Anthropic AML Financial Crimes AI Agent (May 4 BusinessWire)
items.diff_narrative: **New: FIS x Anthropic AML Financial Crimes AI Agent — first Tier-1 banking-platform vendor + frontier-lab joint product with regulatory-grade audit trail; FIS (powers ~12% of global economy) ships Financial Crimes AI Agent that compresses AML alert + case investigations from days to minutes by auto-assembling evidence across a bank's core systems, evaluating activity against known typologies, surfacing highest-risk cases for review; reduces SAR false positives + improves narrative quality; BMO + Amalgamated Bank are first deployment customers, broader availability targeted H2 2026; Anthropic Applied AI team + forward-deployed engineers embedded with FIS; client data stays inside FIS-controlled infrastructure** — Yesterday's report didn't carry FIS at all. Today's drop pairs with the Anthropic 10-agent launch as a banking-vertical pincer move: FIS owns the core-banking distribution rail, Anthropic owns the model + agent harness.

items.headline: Cisco $400M Astrix Security acquisition (May 4)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Cisco $400M Astrix Security acquisition — first major networking-vendor M&A on the agent-identity / non-human-identity (NHI) control-plane primitive; Astrix (founded 2021 by Unit 8200 vets Alon Jackson + Idan Gour) discovers + governs API keys, service accounts, OAuth tokens, machine-to-machine credentials that AI agents now use at scale; integration into Cisco Identity Intelligence, Cisco Secure Access, Duo IAM; Cisco enterprise survey = 85% experimenting with AI agents but only 5% in production; AI agents + non-human identities outnumber human users ~100:1** — Yesterday's report had the Microsoft Agent 365 + Cisco DefenseClaw RSAC stack but no M&A on the NHI category; today reframes the agent-identity convergence as Cisco + Microsoft + Astrix three-vendor lockstep within a single week and makes NHI an explicit M&A primitive.

items.headline: Cloudflare Infire LLM inference engine launch
items.diff_narrative: **New: Cloudflare Infire LLM inference engine — Cloudflare's Rust-based custom inference engine ships across the global edge network, separating input-processing + output-generation onto different optimized systems (prefill/decode disaggregation); 7% faster than vLLM 0.10.0 on unloaded H100 NVL machines, significantly better under real load; targets serving more requests with fewer GPUs and lower CPU overhead; pairs with the standing SGLang vs vLLM 29% throughput gap on H100** — Yesterday's report tracked the SGLang vs vLLM Pareto-split as a two-engine choice. Today adds a third inference-engine candidate with edge-network distribution as the differentiator and reframes the production-serving choice as a three-way Pareto.

items.headline: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 patched (Apr 24 fix carry)
items.diff_narrative: **Updated: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 patched — CVSS 9.8 RCE via /v1/rerank endpoint with malicious GGUF model file; jinja2.Environment() without sandboxing was the root cause; CERT/CC mitigation guidance recommended ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment; reported by Stuart Beck + disclosed Apr 20 via CERT/CC; **fix commit landed Apr 24, jinja2.Environment swapped for ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment**; supply-chain risk vector = malicious GGUF distributed via model repositories** — Yesterday's report had the CVE at ~15 days unpatched as the longest-running 2026 LLM-tooling CVE category. Today closes that disclosure-to-fix window with the patch confirmed in NVD detail; the agentic-AI vulnerability disclosure regime is no longer carrying an open critical.

items.headline: NVIDIA Blackwell B200 hyperscaler deployment scale (May 2026)
items.diff_narrative: **New: NVIDIA Blackwell B200 hyperscaler deployment scale — major cloud providers deploying ~1,000 NVL72 racks per week (each = 72 Blackwell GPUs configured for inference throughput); B200 on-demand rental ~$6.04/hr in May 2026 vs $6.45/hr May 2025 (~6% YoY decline, modest vs steeper analyst forecasts); NVIDIA pre-allocated nearly all B200 production through Q2 2026 with hyperscaler priority (Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta); B200 architecture lowered cost per million tokens 15x vs prior generation (~2c per million tokens on GPT-OSS-120B)** — Yesterday's report didn't carry NVIDIA deployment-scale numbers. Today pairs the ~1,000 NVL72 racks/wk quantification with the AMD Q1 print so the two-vendor AI-accelerator duopoly disclosure shape now has both sides in the public record on the same day.

items.headline: arxiv reasoning-agent + inference-efficiency wave (May 3-5)
items.diff_narrative: **New: arxiv reasoning-agent wave — May 3 paper "Enhanced LLM Reasoning by Optimizing Reward Functions with Search-Driven Reinforcement Learning" (arxiv 2605.02073) treats the reward specification itself as an object of optimization for RL-improved reasoning; May 5 submissions include "EngiAgent: Fully Connected Coordination of LLM Agents for Solving Open-ended Engineering Problems" + "DeGenTWeb: A First Look at LLM-dominant Websites"; "From Laboratory to Real-World Applications: Benchmarking Agentic Code Reasoning at the Repository Level" (arxiv 2601.03731) benchmarks repository-level reasoning across massive interdependent file systems; TRACES + PLP (Progressive Length Prediction) papers target adaptive early-stopping for cost-efficient LRM inference** — Yesterday's report had no fresh arxiv coverage on the reasoning/agent track. Today's 3-day window shows multi-paper convergence on RL-driven reasoning + agentic coordination + adaptive inference-time scaling.
